name: Bermuda Fish Chowder 2 (the Reefs, Southamton, Bermuda)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Grouper, Wahoo, or other firm fish
2 oz sherry peppers (liquid), or substitute 1 chili pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. In a stockpot with 4 quarts water, add the whole fish plus vegetables and herbs for the stock. Bring to a boil, skimming off any scum from the surface.
2. Let simmer for 20 to 25 minutes while you dice the remaining vegetables into half-inch pieces. Strain both the stock and the fish, discarding the skin and bones.
3. In a cleaned stockpot, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the diced vegetables and sauté for about 5 minutes until slightly softened.
4. Gradually add the flour, stirring for about two minutes on low heat to create a roux.
5. Slowly incorporate the fish stock, whisking constantly to achieve a smooth consistency. Bring to a boil and add the tomato paste.
6. Reduce heat and let simmer for 45 to 50 minutes until just before this time has elapsed, gently fold in the cooked fish, sherry peppers (or chili pepper), and rum. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
